notes: Authenticated TaxFlow session bootstrap is working locally with worker runtime coverage.

Stored and recoverable, but spouse-specific fact mapping and validation are still thin.
Collection storage exists, but dependent credit/conflict rules are not yet derived from backend entities.

orchestration: session sync with coarse fact derivation
notes: TaxFlow route state syncs, but W-2 box-level amounts are not yet deterministically mapped into backend facts.
Entity CRUD exists, but backend facts/review logic does not yet unpack 1099 forms into tax-return payloads.
Advanced investment UX exists in TaxFlow, but backend integration stops at persisted screen/entity data.
UI and persistence exist, but QBI, basis, and K-1 facts are not yet mapped into the backend return model.
Rental properties are persisted, but Schedule E fact derivation and review are still backend gaps.
Persisted and restorable, but backend computation hooks for HSA and retirement distributions are still shallow.
Backend has generic storage, but no dedicated facts mapper for these income families yet.
Direct File compatibility exists for many NR scenarios, but TaxFlow-to-backend entity mapping is not complete.
Credit entity loops are strong in UI, but backend review and return payloads do not yet derive credit math from them.
State transfer is wired, but state/local line-item facts are not yet derived from TaxFlow entities.
Checklist and review now come from backend routes instead of static frontend fixtures on the core path.
TaxFlow upload flows now persist document metadata to the worker backend, but OCR/classification is still adapter-backed.
Core TaxFlow e-file submission path is backend-backed and worker-tested end-to-end.
